repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Windows followup of #35223



## Problem
https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/35223#issuecomment-4143161388
`server_stop()` stops listening, but on Windows the named pipe is not immediately reusable. `uv_close()` is asynchronous, and the pipe name may remain unavailable until after the old server exits and the OS finishes releasing it. If the new server tries to restore the old `--listen` address too early, restart can fail.
## Solution
The new server now starts on a temporary
address and receives the original address via restart handoff.
It then retries `serverstart()` from startup until the canonical address becomes available and all ui's done migrating or grace period for migration is exceeded, then it closes the
temporary listener.

comment_data.body: might as well retry on all platforms, it's harmless. but keep the comment about Windows.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -262,11 +262,15 @@ describe('TUI :restart', function()
     local server_session = n.connect(server_pipe)
     local _, server_pid = server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'getpid', {})
     local function assert_new_pid()
+      server_session:close()
       if is_os('win') then
-        return -- FIXME
+        -- On Windows, --listen address is restored async (after old server exits).
+        retry(nil, 5000, function()
+          server_session = n.connect(server_pipe)
+        end)
+      else
+        server_session = n.connect(server_pipe)

comment_data.body: rather than add another ui-event arg, can we somehow fixup things in the server-side? we were planning to do the `command` part here anyway, which may then give more flexibility in this server-side logic for deciding what `listen_addr` to send to the ui client.
I disagree. Having to retry on non-Windows is a regression.

comment_data.body:
Dropping the `NULL` check doesn't affect the value of `listen_arg`. It usually shouldn't be NULL anyway.
```suggestion
    if (di != NULL && di->di_tv.v_type == VAR_STRING) {
```

I'm not sure if stopping the new server's initial listening address is a good idea. It's sent in the "restart" UI event, while `UIEnter` is triggered when the first UI attaches. After #34367 is addressed there may be other UIs that haven't attached yet.

can we do this logic in Lua? if you add a function to `runtime/lua/vim/_core/server.lua` you can call it like this: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/df8d98173cbc03d78da18fee7f31f130db2c9e44/src/nvim/eval/funcs.c#L6334

is `nvim__set_restart_handoff` this actually needed, or could we call Lua here?

    rpc_send_call(..., "nvim_exec_lua", ...)

to avoid a big old Lua string here, it may be convenient to [define most of the logic in `_core/server.lua`](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38539#discussion_r3075956511).

comment_regular_data.body:
> The new server now starts on a temporary address and receives the original address ... then retries `serverstart()` until the canonical address becomes available 

This makes sense! Can you put this outline in one place, probably in `ex_restart`, so that we have a single place where the approach can be understood.

The general approach makes sense, but hopefully most of the logic can live in Lua.

title: fix(:restart): --listen reusage on windows #35223
